Plaintiff Roger Dennis'S Motion To Compel Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Aug-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Christine M. Schenone for plaintiff and moving party: Roger Dennis, Darla Dennis, Roger Dennnis and Mitchell Dennis
Christiane Sinclair for defendant and opposing party: California State Automobile Association
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.
Interrogatories
8:
Denied. The objections are overruled and stricken. The answer is otherwise sufficient.
9: Granted. The objections, other than based on the attorney client and attorney work product privileges are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond for the period of September 29, 2002 to September 29, 2004. The last name and other personal identifying information of an insured shall be redacted. If any documents are withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log. If after production, plaintiff seeks discovery of the identities of any complainants, the parties shall meet and confer as to following the procedures outlined in Colonial Life and Accident Ins. Co. v. Superior Court.
10: Granted in part. The objections are overruled and stricken. The last name and other personal identifying information of an insured shall be redacted. If after production, plaintiff seeks discovery of the identities of any complainants, the parties shall meet and confer as to following the procedures outlined in Colonial Life and Accident Ins. Co. v. Superior Court.
Defendant is ordered to provide supplemental responses and produce documents on or before September 29, 2006. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

16: Granted. The objections, other than based on the attorney client and attorney work product privileges are overruled and stricken.
